purpose: Census of everything the Electron shell asar/extraResources actually ship, plus which script (if any) produces each source. build:shell (scripts/build-shell.mjs) and its guard test (tests/shell-surface-manifest.test.ts) both key off this file so an unreviewed change to package.json's electron-builder `files`/`extraResources` fails a test instead of silently drifting from what build:shell knows how to build.

shellNativeDependencies.name: ws
shellNativeDependencies.reason: vite.config.main.js keeps "ws" external from main.bundle.cjs (comment: "CJS native addon (bufferutil/utf-8-validate) breaks when bundled") — the module is required at runtime from node_modules, not inlined. package.json build.files re-includes "node_modules/ws/**" after the blanket "!**/node_modules/**" exclusion so it lands in the asar.

asarFiles.note: vite build entry desktop/src/splash.html, plus a closeBundle plugin (copySplashRuntimeAssets -> scripts/splash-assets.mjs copySplashAssets) that copies modules/platform.js, all locales/*.json, and the yuan-avatar PNGs named by shared/yuan-visuals.ts YUAN_VISUALS into dist-splash/. splash is the one shell-owned HTML surface: it must render before any MIKO_HOME artifact is unpacked, so it cannot depend on dist-renderer build output.

Recursive glob; includes desktop/src/*.html (raw source HTML, used by loadPageFromDir()'s fallback path when a dist-renderer build for that page isn't present), icon files, and theme-registry-data.json. Not a build product.

This is the file app.getVersion() reads inside the packaged asar (electron-builder copies it in, with extraMetadata.main overriding only the `main` field — `version` passes through unmodified). Do not confuse with a hypothetical separate shell-version file; there isn't one, and this ticket does not create one.

asarFiles.producedBy: static (hand-authored per release, see AGENTS/CLAUDE release-digest workflow)

extraResources.note: build:shell never runs build:server and never reads MIKO_SIGN_KEY. It only runs scripts/verify-seed-kit.mjs (fail-closed) against whatever already exists at dist-server-artifact/{os}-{arch}/; a missing/mismatched kit is a hard build:shell failure with a message pointing at `npm run build:server` or fetching an already-signed artifact.

Same source tree as the asarFiles desktop/src/assets/** entry above; electron-builder ships it both inside the asar and unpacked under Resources/assets/. Not this ticket's concern to redesign — recorded as-is.
Swift build, mac-only (shouldBuildComputerUseHelper() gates on process.platform === 'darwin'). build:shell runs this step conditionally, mirroring the existing pack/dist scripts.

vendor/mingit is gitignored and only extracted by Windows pack CI (build.yml runs download-mingit.js then smoke-mingit.mjs). The forward guard asserts the downloader script exists — never the extracted tree — so plain npm test on windows-latest does not require a provisioned MinGit checkout. scripts/smoke-mingit.mjs documents the runtime contract the extracted tree must satisfy at pack time.

MSVC (cl.exe) build, win32-only (shouldBuildWindowsSandboxHelper() gates on process.platform === 'win32'). Cannot run on a non-Windows host and is out of scope for the build:shell step list in this ticket (dist:win already builds it as a separate pre-build:client step); recorded here purely for manifest completeness so the guard test's reverse-coverage check passes.

buildHooks.note: Validates the seed/ four-piece kit landed in extraResources and patches asar production deps / node_modules/.bin symlinks. Does not read MIKO_SIGN_KEY.

Skipped locally via SKIP_NOTARIZE=true (same convention as npm run install:local). electron-builder's --dir target does not invoke afterSign at all in practice since there is no code-signed artifact to notarize, but the hook itself is a no-op without Apple credentials regardless.

buildShellSteps: verify:seed-kit (scripts/verify-seed-kit.mjs; fail-closed against an existing dist-server-artifact/{os}-{arch}/; never triggers build:server)

themeAttribution.reason: build:theme's own outDir is literally under dist-renderer/ (desktop/dist-renderer/lib/theme.js). Its only consumers are <script src="lib/theme.js"> tags in index.html, mobile.html, settings.html, quick-chat.html, onboarding.html, and browser-viewer.html — all six are vite.config.ts renderer build inputs (rollupOptions.input), none of which ship in the asar or as extraResources. desktop/src/splash.html — the one shell-owned HTML surface — has its own inline <style> block and does not reference lib/theme.js at all. Conclusion: build:theme's product belongs entirely to the renderer artifact pipeline; build:shell must not run it.
evidence: grep 'lib/theme.js' desktop/src/*.html -> matches index.html, mobile.html, browser-viewer.html, onboarding.html, settings.html, quick-chat.html; no match in splash.html
vite.config.ts rollupOptions.input lists exactly those six HTML files as renderer entries

excludedFromShell.reason: Renderer is not shipped inside the shell asar. It's a separately versioned artifact that build:server packs into the signed seed kit (dist-server-artifact/{os}-{arch}/); the shell resolves an activated renderer directory at runtime from MIKO_HOME/artifacts, never from Resources directly except via the seed archive. build:shell never invokes build:renderer.

Same seed-kit story as build:renderer — server ships as a signed archive inside the seed kit, not as loose files in the shell package. build:shell only verifies a pre-existing kit (scripts/verify-seed-kit.mjs); it never builds or signs one.
See themeAttribution above — its product is a renderer-only asset.
